review_text: I love this game, it's really F2P friendly, and it doesn't push you to spend money in-game, the graphics are pretty, the story is amazing, and my phone doesn't even overheat, the only possible bad thing (but it's really not that bad) is that you can't have off-piece artifacts, and the bosses gain more and more health, but it really pushes you to make your builds better and better, which is understandable, so yeah, overall it's a really good game, and it's not too laggy on mobile either :D

They have added a character that can revive your entire team even if she herself isn't on the team. The last four star character came out in September 2024. Every patch they add two five stars and every DPS is stronger than the last. Important story beats are resolved off screen (2.1's ending). They intentionally give characters big flaws just to sell you their E2 or lightcone/weapon. this is an updated review on this game from 2023. I really adore this game ever since I started playing it. It's fast paced, easy to play. The events are decent, not too bad. You can apply autobattle while battling the enemies on the game which for me is really convenient because I get to do my work. Furthermore, I also like the songs in this game. It's both melancholy and blessed. Not to brag, also, about the designs in this game, it's pretty well made. Overall, 9/10.
2 years later and my review still stands. Rigged gacha, bad combat system, non-existent elemental reaction, bad 4 star rooster. inflexible party build and horrendous powercreep

The story is fun, and it's pretty easy to get into as a new player. However, once you've done the main story, it gets boring quickly. There are no new companion missions, no new 4 star characters. Every patch has a new character the meta caters to and it becomes near-impossible to clear late game content with old characters unless they're highly invested. The gambling aspect infects every part of the game, and there isn't nearly enough to do in-game for free to play players to earn pulls.

Update: I really loved this game when it first came out, but with how fast power creep is, you have to spend money to keep relevant. The good: Weird, philosophical storytelling. Good music. Doesn't mind letting its characters be goofy, especially in event stories. Excellent animations. Voice acting is generally good. The bad: The higher tiers of the end-game modes Memory of Chaos and Apocalyse may become inaccessible for free to play and light spenders. More archetypes are being released, so building a specific team may take a long time with the rotating banner. Gear grind gets old. Way more predatory tactics with 3.7 release

Game looks good visually, but is not worth your time in the end. This game focuses way too much on "teams" instead of individual characters. Meaning that if you like a specific character, but don't necessarily like the "team" that the developers intended that character to be played with, you'd be better off just not picking up that character at all, because their performance will likely be drastically reduced outside of that team. I won't even speak on the horrible monetization practices.
